description.text:
Bumps [electron](https://github.com/electron/electron) from 38.3.0 to 39.0.0.
Release notes

Sourced from electron's releases.

electron v39.0.0

Release Notes for v39.0.0

Fixes

  • Fixed a potential crash in dialog.showOpenDialog on Windows with an empty extension filter array. #48658 (Also in 37, 38)
  • Fixed an error when checking environmental variables when using remote checksums when installing Electron via npm. #48673 (Also in 37, 38)

Other Changes

  • Updated Chromium to 142.0.7444.52. #48641

electron v39.0.0-beta.5

Note: This is a beta release. Please file new issues for any bugs you find in it.

This release is published to npm under the beta tag and can be installed via npm install electron@beta, or npm install electron@39.0.0-beta.5.

Release Notes for v39.0.0-beta.5

Features

  • Added methods to enable more granular accessibility support management. #48625
  • Added the ability to retrieve the system accent color on Linux using systemPreferences.getAccentColor. #48628

electron v39.0.0-beta.4

Note: This is a beta release. Please file new issues for any bugs you find in it.

This release is published to npm under the beta tag and can be installed via npm install electron@beta, or npm install electron@39.0.0-beta.4.

Release Notes for v39.0.0-beta.4

Features

  • Added RGBAF16 output format with scRGB HDR color space support to Offscreen Rendering. #48504
  • Support dynamic ESM imports in non-context isolated preloads. #48488 (Also in 37, 38)

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue where systemPreferences.getAccentColor inverted the color. #48624
  • Fixed an issue where trafficLightPosition would not be correctly applied when using titleBarStyle: 'customButtonsOnHover' on macOS 26. #48621 (Also in 38)
  • Fixed an issue where button background on mouse hover with titleBarOverlay wasn't always calculated to provide appropriate minimum contrast. #48597 (Also in 37)
  • Fixed an issue where calling webContents.openDevTools({ mode: 'detach' }) would cause a crash on Wayland. #48623
  • Fixed an issue where icons didn't show up as expected on Windows Toast notifications. #48629 (Also in 38)
  • Fixed shader-f16 to work on Windows. #48557 (Also in 37, 38)
  • Fixed white flash on call to BrowserWindow.show. #48559 (Also in 37, 38)
  • Titlebar buttons now appear on the correct side of windows on Wayland in Ubuntu. #48603 (Also in 38)

Other Changes

  • Updated Chromium to 142.0.7444.34. #48571

electron v39.0.0-beta.3

Note: This is a beta release. Please file new issues for any bugs you find in it.

... (truncated)

Commits
  • 0abda74 docs: modify the thickFrame doc (#48677)
  • 4e8a552 fix: logical bug in install.js env var handling (#48673)
  • d83383b docs: fix Ubuntu version used to build Electron (#48643)
  • 496db94 chore: bump chromium to 142.0.7444.52 (39-x-y) (#48641)
  • 00627c6 fix: crash on empty dialog extensions array on Windows (#48658)
  • 7319e5c docs: security.md mark 'Enable process sandboxing' as active by defau… (#48647)
  • 1056280 feat: enable more granular a11y feature management (#48625)
  • 4fda94b feat: Add getAccentColor on Linux (#48628)
  • e3715b0 fix: systemPreferences.getAccentColor inverted color (#48624)
  • 90674e0 fix: icon in Windows toast notification (#48629)
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

You can also trigger a rebase manually by commenting `@dependabot rebase`. [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-start) [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-end) ---
Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot show ignore conditions` will show all of the ignore conditions of the specified dependency - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself)

CC @benniekiss 

In this issue, I want to discuss and then manage actionable items to improve the footnote handling workflow.

## Problem Description

The problem that this issue shall solve is that footnotes are a bit tricky. Footnotes consist of the footnote itself (a marker where it should be inserted) and the reference (the content of the footnote). In traditional word processors, this can be easily achieved simply by inserting a footnote node at the appropriate position and then handle it as required. For Markdown, however, this is more difficult, because a footnote consists of two semi-independent elements that must be added manually, and whose labels need to be explicitly specified.

This is usually less of an issue, but as the amount of footnotes grows, it becomes difficult to manage them due to the constant back and forth.

Zettlr can, and should, make this easier by automating whatever it can.

## Conceptualizing What to Automate

The first question is: what CAN Zettlr automate? The checked checkboxes indicate what it already does

* [ ] Relabelling footnotes ascending
* [ ] Moving footnotes to the end of the document
* [x] Inserting both a footnote label and reference automatically
* [x] Jumping between footnote label and reference
* [ ] Deleting the label when the reference is deleted, and vice versa.
* More...?

The last and first points where what I tried to add in the filter that led to the performance issues @benniekiss observed, and it was in general finicky.

The issue is: we need to know when to do any of that.

## When to Automate

This automation is a balance between responsiveness and performance. Clearly, we can't do that on every keypress via a filter. Both, because it has an enormous performance impact, and because it makes working much more difficult because it can fully delete information which leads to data loss.

Thus, I propose do always run these as commands after other commands. Then we only need to know which commands are relevant. I can think of the following:

* Insertion of a new footnote via shortcut
* Deletion of a footnote via shortcut (not yet implemented, but this would be a great addition)
* pasting text (if a footnote is detected)

This would ensure that users can still manually tinker and that nothing can break so easily, while still making the footnote workflow simpler.

## Edge Cases

I can currently think of the following edge cases:

* Inline footnotes (where label and reference is the same)
* Non-numerical footnote labels
* Duplicate labels and/or references

## Other potential improvements

* Add references to copied markdown text if it contains their labels
* Remove references on cutting if the text contains their labels and add them to the cut text. (Can be an additional step after what copying does)

## Anything else?

summary: Adjusting `o` newline key in vim mode to retain bullet point formatting.

description.text:
### Description

In normal mode with vim mode on, pressing `o` creates a new line. The only problem with this is that if you are writing a bullet list, pressing `o` does not create a new bullet so you have to manually add it back. Pressing `o` at the moment does at least keep the tabbing the same (e.g., a nest group of bullets doesn't require adding spaces to get the tabbing back before adding 2., *, - or other bullet marker), so its not too bad.

### Proposed Changes

I think the proposed changes are simple. Add a new line in [this file](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/blob/a1c5438acfdfd828ff1aac2f553ad874b5fef6e4/source/common/modules/markdown-editor/plugins/vim-mode.ts#L105C1-L105C19) to map `o` to `A-Enter`. `A` moves the cursor to the end of the line and enters insert mode. Enter creates a new line and retains the bullet formatting on this new line.

### Caveats

I can't think of any downsides. I've tested comparing the functionality of `o` on a paragraph (with many wrapped lines), in the middle of a list of numbered bullets, on a bullet like - or *, and in each case I found that `A-enter` resulted in the same behaviour or improved the process, compared to `o`.

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

Yes

### Zettlr Version

Stable (most recent version)

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Operating System Version

Windows 10, Mac OS 15.7.1, Ubuntu LTS (not sure of the version on the top of my head but it should be up to date)

### Additional Information

I don't have much experience in any of the languages used in Zettlr. But it appears like a simple process to implement and try out (adding one line of code using vim.map command).

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR refactors both inline and block math parsers, and removes the `partialParse` function, which is no longer used in the codebase.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
The inline math parser was refactored to use delimiters.

The block math parser was refactored for readability. I don't believe the logic is changed.

A check was added to the math renderer `createWidget` function to first check if the cursor is within or adjacent to a math node. This improves UX when cursoring next to inline or block math content.

The `partial-parse` module was removed as its no longer used.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 26
